posts.title: Under The Cherry Blossoms – Chapter 5: Pink-Haired Lady
posts.excerpt: "So, this is Polaris — our not-so-finished AI marvel that can distinguish between two different entities in an image. It's remarkable, don't you think?" Ethan begins, showcasing the slide on the projection board. Lily, her eyes wide with intrigue, nods as she scribbles down notes, "Incredible indeed
posts.content:
"So, this is Polaris — our not-so-finished AI marvel that can distinguish between two different entities in an image. It's remarkable, don't you think?" Ethan begins, showcasing the slide on the projection board.

Lily, her eyes wide with intrigue, nods as she scribbles down notes, "Incredible indeed. But from what you've explained so far, doesn't it already possess all the capabilities required for the Raviga project?"

Days turn into weeks, with Lily continuing her training under Ethan's mentorship. He familiarizes her with all the products developed by Mystique Holdings, detailing their uses and applications. He also shares more information about her project, Raviga. Throughout these sessions, she finds herself increasingly attracted to the charismatic Ethan.

Simultaneously, she notices a peculiar phenomenon. Almost every day upon arriving at the office, she sees a pink-haired lady standing outside the building. At first, she assumes the woman must be a local resident, so she decides to overlook her presence.

“Raviga is a large company, so they're expecting a fully developed product. As Polaris is new and still in development, we can't showcase it yet.” Ethan replies.

“But what if we…” Lily begins, outlining her idea to Ethan who listens attentively, a small smile playing on his lips. “With this approach, I doubt they will reject us.” She finishes her proposal confidently.

“This is impressive, Lily, but we don’t have a sufficient budget to implement this on such a large scale,” Ethan tells her.

“Given that Raviga is a major company, if the tech team somehow manages to develop this before the deadline, I think you guys can negotiate a higher fee, considering there's nothing like this in the market.” Lily suggests. She continues, “Also, I've examined the project and product reports you shared with me in the previous sessions. There are several projects where we're spending more budget than necessary. I could come up with a revised budget report that might be more efficient and help us manage the Raviga project.”

Ethan smiles again, saying, “I must admit, this seems like a creative solution. Alright, Lily, present your report to me tomorrow and I'll see if we can implement it.”

Lily nods and returns his smile.

"Alright Lily, I have a meeting in about..." Ethan glances at his Rolex watch, his brow furrowing in confusion. "Oh wow, it seems like I do have a bit of time."

Lily silently chuckles at this and asks, "Ethan, may I ask a question?"

Ethan shifts his gaze to her and replies, "Of course, Lily. What's on your mind?"

"You're always busy, aren't you?" Lily inquires, prompting Ethan to respond with a bemused "Hm?"

"What I mean is," Lily continues, "you're the owner and manager of such a significant company. You must be overwhelmed with meetings and other responsibilities."

Ethan nods and admits, "That's true. My schedule is usually packed."

With that in mind, Lily can't help but wonder, "Then why do you still take time out for these training sessions?" Realizing her question might sound impertinent, she quickly adds, "Couldn't someone else in the company take over that role?"

Ethan regards her for a moment, and Lily worries she may have upset him. But then he smiles and begins to explain. "It's a bit of a long story," he starts. "When I first founded this company, we didn't have many people. In fact, we barely had anyone at all, only Frederick and Viktor. But as time passed, we started to grow. People like Jessica, Melissa, and so on joined the team. You probably met most of them at your welcome party."

"I remember," Lily chimes in, her mind failing to summon images of Viktor or Jessica from the party. Yet, the figure of the tall, imposing Frederick does emerge distinctly from her memories. She decides to hold her tongue, quietly absorbing the rest of Ethan's story.

"I was the one who trained and mentored them in the early days," Ethan continues. "As we started developing various products, I began to feel that, as the creator of these products, it was my responsibility to instruct the employees about them."

He pauses, reflecting on the journey of his company. "As the company grew, we had people join and leave, but no matter what, every single employee has been trained by me at the start of their journey here. I might not be able to supervise everyone all the time, but at least in the beginning..." He trails off, realizing he might have shared more than necessary.

Listening to Ethan, Lily finds herself filled with respect for him. "That's amazing," she murmurs, not quite realizing she's voiced her thoughts aloud.

"Thank you," Ethan replies, his gaze meeting hers.

Ethan and Lily shared an intense moment of eye contact before both simultaneously realize the closeness. Ethan stammers, stepping back and fumbling over his words. "Well okay, Miss Andrew," he straightens himself up, managing to force a professional demeanor. "I will see you later, have a good day ahead."

"You too, Ethan," Lily replies, holding his gaze for just a moment longer. As he leaves, she's left alone with her thoughts, her curiosity and admiration for Ethan blossoming into a deeper attraction.

As the days go by, Lily throws herself into her work, delivering a stellar budget report for the Raviga project that impresses even Ethan. Her dedication doesn't go unnoticed, and she finds herself becoming part of the work family, sharing lunches and laughs with Layla, Melissa, and Liam.

However, she couldn't help but notice the recurrent appearance of the pink-haired woman outside the office building. Her curiosity piqued, Lily decided to bring it up with Layla one day.

"Layla, who is this pink-haired lady?" Lily asks curiously.

"Oh, that's the landlady," Layla replies.

"The landlady?" Lily repeats, surprised.

"Yes, she's the owner of the building," Layla explains, pondering the situation.

"Huh, she looks so young, maybe a bit older than you," Lily expresses her astonishment.

"Haha, I don't know. I've never spoken to her, but this town certainly has talented young people," Layla chuckles.

"Well, I see her here every day at this time," Lily mentions.

"Oh yes, she's always here at this time. I think she checks on her building, but I don't really know," Layla responds.

"Oh, okay. That seems weird, but sure," Lily says, still feeling a bit confused.

"Don't worry too much about it," Layla reassures her.

"Do you know her name, though?" Lily asks, hoping for more information.

"Hmm, I can't really remember," Layla ponders.

"Aren't you the receptionist of this place and still don't know the name of the landlady?" Lily smirks playfully.

"Oh, shush. I'm trying to remember. I think it's Miss Hayes or something," Layla tries to recall.

"Miss Hayes, I see," Lily finds it intriguing.

Just then, Melissa appears and says, "I'd be careful if I were you, Lily."

"Huh? I'm sorry, Miss Stone?" Lily is confused by Melissa's warning.

"I'm just saying, it's better if you stay away from that pink witch. It won't lead to any good," Melissa bluntly advises.

"Umm..." Lily becomes even more perplexed by Melissa's words.

"Just remember what I said," Melissa concludes and heads to her office.

"What was that all about?" Lily asks Layla, seeking some clarity.

"Even I don't know what's up with grumpy," Layla replies, equally puzzled by Melissa's behavior.

Lily eventually returns to her desk, where Liam greets her. After a brief conversation, Lily makes her way to the conference room for the training session, where Ethan continues to impart knowledge and guide her through the intricacies of their project Raviga. The session proceeds smoothly, with Lily absorbing the information eagerly and asking thoughtful questions. Her enthusiasm and dedication impress Ethan, who commends her progress and encourages her to keep up the good work

Ethan finished the training and leaned back in his chair, rubbing his eyes a bit before looking at Lily. "So, are you enjoying your new workplace, Lily?" He asked, the corners of his mouth lifting in a faint smile.

Lily blinked in surprise, as Ethan usually didn’t do small talk. After a moment's pause, she replied, "Yes, I actually really like it here. I'm learning a lot of new things every day."

"I'm glad, Lily." Ethan responded, his voice calm and sincere. He nodded in appreciation, and Lily couldn't help but return the smile.

She tilted her head slightly, her eyes twinkling with playful curiosity. "So, another meeting today?"

Ethan laughed - a deep, hearty sound - and shook his head. "Unfortunately, yes." He sighed, running a hand through his hair. "It's difficult to find free time with so many meetings."

"I don’t think you actually would have free time anyway." Lily teased, a hint of laughter in her voice.

Ethan feigned shock at her comment, his eyes widening theatrically. "That's not true! I do get free time." He insisted, chuckling.

Lily bit her lip, hesitating for a moment. "So, if you don’t mind, can I ask another question?"

"Sure, Lily. And don’t be so formal." Ethan responded, gesturing for her to continue.

She inhaled, gathering her thoughts. "Does your work actually leave you with time for anything outside work?" Lily asked, her tone more serious.

Ethan paused, caught off guard by her straightforward question. He blinked a couple of times, then chuckled awkwardly, "Umm... ehh?"

Realizing she might have crossed a line, Lily quickly clarified, "I mean, a hobby." She frowned, thinking to herself, "Hobby, who the hell even asks about hobbies these days?”

“Do you have any hobbies or anything?" She blurted out, instantly regretting her word choice.

Unfortunately, Ethan didn't have an answer to either of Lily's questions. Except for work, he didn’t really do anything else. He paused, his brow furrowing as he wracked his brain for a suitable response. When nothing immediately came to mind, he glanced down at his watch and swiftly stood up.

“Oh, I have to call Mr. Raymond. I'm sorry, Lily, but let's continue our conversation some other time? You did well in the training today. I'll see you soon." His words rushed out in a hasty tumble.

With that, Ethan turned on his heel and strode towards the door, leaving Lily alone in the conference room. She stared at his retreating back, a look of confusion etched on her face as she wondered whether she had crossed a line.

To Be Continued on chapter 6

posts.title: Under The Cherry Blossoms – Chapter 4 : Cafeteria

She reaches her desk and there, leaning against the cubicle partition, is Liam. "Training with Ethan... took a while, huh?" His voice is ca
posts.content:
Lily, backpack slung over one shoulder, heads towards her workspace, weaving her way through the clumps of colleagues trickling towards the cafeteria for lunch. She reaches her desk and there, leaning against the cubicle partition, is Liam.

"Training with Ethan... took a while, huh?" His voice is casual but the trace of an unasked question hangs in the air.

Lily, placing her bag down, responds with a lighthearted laugh. "Sure did, but it wasn't time wasted."

Liam's lips curl up into a small smile, a slight blush creeping onto his cheeks. "I, um... waited for you. Lunch?"

Her response is a bright grin. "You waited? That's sweet, thank you."

Just as they are about to make their move to the cafeteria, a sudden burst of energy in the form of Layla's voice echoes through the room.

"Lilyyyy!" Layla, with her trademark enthusiasm, throws her arms around Lily in an affectionate squeeze. "Time for lunch, let's go!"

Lily exchanges a surprised glance with Liam. "You waited too, Layla?"

"Naturally! I knew you were in training. And... 'too'? Was someone else waiting?" Layla, feigning innocence, looks curiously at Liam.

"Uh, Layla." Liam greets, his voice slightly strained.

Unfazed, Layla links her arm with Lily's, "Why not all four of us go for lunch?"

Lily and Liam exchange a glance, both echoing the question, "Four?"

"With grumpy, of course!" Layla gestures towards the entrance where Melissa stands with a bemused expression.

The realization of Melissa's inclusion draws a laugh from Lily and a muttered, "Great, Melissa too," from Liam, almost inaudible.

"What was that?" Layla turns towards Liam.

"Hmm? Nothing. Lunch, right?" Liam tries to dismiss the question, while Layla's infectious excitement begins to drag Lily away.

Catching Liam's eye, Lily's smile silently encourages him to follow. His gaze lingers a moment longer on Lily before he grumbles to himself, "Oh, Stupid Layla."

The group - Lily, Liam, Layla, and Melissa - thread their way to the cafeteria, a bustling hub of activity and welcome change from the quiet efficiency of their tenth floor workspace. The air buzzes with the chatter of their colleagues and is infused with the mouthwatering aroma of diverse food. After selecting their meals, they slip into seats at their favorite corner table.

"Wow, this cafeteria never fails to surprise me with its variety of options!" Layla exclaims, her eyes wide with delight as she takes in the plethora of food in front of her.

"That's true," Liam nods in agreement, "The range of choices here is amazing."

Melissa lets out a small sigh as she stirs her soup. "Well, Ethan has always said that providing good food to employees leads to better performance."

"But I've never seen him eating here," Layla observes, looking around the cafeteria as if expecting to spot Ethan.

"That's because he's a fancy bitch," Melissa replies, her tone playful.

The table erupts in laughter, even as Lily and Liam widen their eyes at the unexpected comment.

"Oh my god, did I just hear our HR cursing?" Layla giggles, her eyes sparkling with mirth.

Melissa grins, unrepentant, "Relax, we're not in the office right now."

"Technically, this café is part of the office building, owned by Mystique Holdings," Layla counters, matching Melissa's grin with her own.

Melissa throws up her hands in surrender, "Alright, you win."

The banter continues, though Lily is quiet, her gaze far-off as she gets lost in her thoughts about Ethan.

Breaking the silence, Liam pitches in, "I've seen him going to the fancy restaurant downstairs. Sometimes during lunch, I go to Starbucks below our building, and I see him walking outside of it."

Melissa nods, "Oh yeah, that's exactly where he eats."

Layla's eyes widen in disbelief, "No way, that place is too expensive for me. I can't afford to eat there even once."

Melissa merely shrugs, "Just as I said, he's fancy. Trust me, I know him from a long time."

Liam adds, "That restaurant is amazing, though. It's right in front of our building and has such a beautiful ambiance."

"But I doubt anyone would eat there every day," Liam replies, his eyebrows furrowed in thought.

Melissa shoots Liam a smirk, "Didn't we already establish that our manager is fancy?"

Suddenly noticing Lily's quiet demeanor, Layla asks, "And Lily, why were you so quiet? What's been on your mind?"

Melissa and Liam turn to Lily with concerned expressions. "Are you alright, Lily?" Melissa asks gently.

"I'm fine, Miss Stone," Lily responds with a smile, albeit a bit distracted.

Melissa, not wanting to push, decides to change the topic. "Okay, Lily, moving on. How was your first day of training? Any highlights?"

"It went really well," Lily replies, her face lighting up at the question. "I learned so much and felt engaged throughout the session."

Layla's face splits into a wide grin, "I knew you would excel!"

Liam offers, "Remember, if you need any assistance or support, don't hesitate to reach out."

"Thank you, Liam. I appreciate that," Lily responds, her face brightening even further.

Once they finish their meal, they all head back to the office, ready to dive back into work.

As the day's work draws to a close, Lily gathers her reports and makes her way to Ethan's office. The low hum of computers and quiet conversations are a stark contrast to the usual office hustle. She finds Ethan immersed in his work, fingers flying over his keyboard. She raps lightly on the door, and his voice, slightly muffled by the partition, beckons her in.

"Come in, Lily," Ethan intones, without looking up.

"I thought I would drop off my reports before heading home," Lily explains, holding up the stack of papers in her hand.

Ethan finally pauses his typing and looks up, blinking owlishly, "You could've shown them to me tomorrow, you know."

"I know, but I finished them, so why wait?" Lily offers with a small smile, laying the papers on his desk.

Ethan's brows furrow in surprise, "Wait, you've finished the Raviga report?"

"I did. It was a bit challenging, but I got through it," Lily nods, a note of pride entering her voice.

"That's a three-day task, Lily. You've done well, but remember not to overdo it. It's your first day," Ethan advises, leaning back in his chair, his gaze never leaving her face.

Lily's smile widens at his words, "Well, they say first impressions are the most important."

"And you've definitely made an impressive one," Ethan concedes with an appreciative nod.

Leaving the reports on his table, Lily bids him a quiet farewell and leaves, the silence of the office engulfing her once more. posts.title: Audrey -- Chapter 3 : The Morning After
posts.excerpt: Oh my god, what have I done. I wake up naked with Evans Carter sleeping beside me. Good job, Audrey. Meet a guy once, then let him in your bed. Real proud moment. He’s out cold, breathing easy, looking way too damn cute. Not the point. The point is: how the hell did he even win me over? It’s barely
posts.content:
Oh my god, what have I done.

I wake up naked with Evans Carter sleeping beside me. Good job, Audrey. Meet a guy once, then let him in your bed. Real proud moment.

He’s out cold, breathing easy, looking way too damn cute. Not the point. The point is: how the hell did he even win me over?
It’s barely seven. Too early. Normally I’d sneak out after a hookup, but… it’s my own apartment. Not much sneaking possible when he’s here.

Not only did Evans manage to charm me, he stayed the night. In my bed. In my space. Am I really that easy? No. Definitely not. This is an exception. A hot exception, but still.

I slip out of bed and head to the shower. Hot water, vanilla lotion, hair dryer, the usual ritual. By the time I step back out, Evans is stirring.
He cracks one eye open. “Mornin’.”
“Morning, sunshine,” I say, blasting the dryer.
“What time is it?”
“Eight, maybe.”
He groans and flops face-first into the pillow. “Too early. Why are you awake?”
“This isn’t a hotel, Carter. Get up.”
“Shhh. Five more minutes.”
I laugh. “Fine. But don’t blame me for the noise.”

Sure enough, the dryer does its job and he sits up, yawning.
“Sorry. Not sorry,” I grin.
He stretches. “Early shift?”
“Nope. Don’t go in until noon.”
“Then why torture yourself waking up this early?”
“Because some of us function like normal humans,” I shoot back. “Also, anyone would’ve woken up after last night.”

That earns me a smirk. He slides closer, bold as hell, and cups my ass like it’s the most natural thing in the world.
“Really?” I deadpan.
“You know,” he murmurs against my neck, “we could pick up where we left off…”
I step aside. His lips hang open.
“Listen, Evans. Last night was fun.”
He cuts me off, mocking my tone: “But it can’t happen again because blah blah blah…”
“Hey!” I swat him with the dryer.
“That’s what you were gonna say, right?”
“Well… duh.” I pout, still fussing with my hair.

“Didn’t you enjoy it?” he asks.
I glance at him, cheeks heating. “I did. Okay? It was fun.”
“Then what’s the problem?”
“It was a hookup, Evans. That’s it. I don’t even know how it happened, but it doesn’t need to happen again.”

Before I can step away, he grabs me, spins me toward him, and stares right into my eyes.
“You loved it,” he says quietly.
I swallow, try to look away. “I… I did.”
He grins and kisses me. Deep. Hungry. And damn it, I kiss him back before my brain catches up. I melt instantly… then shove him off.
“Evans!” My voice comes out louder than I meant.
He blinks, confused.
“It’s not happening again.” This time, I say it firm.

~

So yeah. Judge me again. Morning after, round two. What is it with this guy?
Fuck.

Again, I end up in the shower. Same hot water, same vanilla lotion, same “what the hell am I doing with my life” thoughts. Round two in less than twelve hours.
When I come out, Evans is half-dressed, pulling on his jacket.
“Done with your shower?” he asks.
“Sharp as ever,” I deadpan. “You know, you could actually use it too. Soap works wonders.”
He laughs. “I’ll shower at home, love. Running late.”

That love word sticks weird in my chest. Not used to that.
“Something happen?” I ask, eyebrow raised.
“Yeah. Long story. I’ll tell you sometime.”
“Right.”
He smirks. “Aw, you look sad I’m leaving.”
“You wish. Been waiting for you to leave since morning.” I roll my eyes.
“Sure you have.” He steps closer.
I turn my head. “Fine, whatever. It was… a good time.”

Before I can finish, he grabs me, pulls me in, and kisses me—soft, uninvited, like he owns the moment. And damn it, I kiss him back.
When he finally lets go, he murmurs, “Don’t say it’s over.”
“We’re not done?” I ask, arching a brow.
“Of course not.”

He smiles, warm enough to make me forget myself for half a second.
I snap out of it. “Listen, Evans—”
“You’re not looking for a relationship?” he finishes.
“…Well, yeah.”
“I know.”
“Uh… what?”
“Relax. I can still come to the bar as your friend, right?”
“I guess.” Yeah, sure. Friends. Friends don’t usually shove their tongues down your throat.
“Good enough for me.” He grins.

His grin is stupid. Stupid and warm.
“You just want sex,” I shoot back.
“True. But that doesn’t mean I don’t want you.”

Okay… yeah. That one hits. I blush like an idiot.
“Don’t worry,” he adds with a wink. “I’ll try to keep it no-strings. Maybe.”

I laugh, but it’s nervous. Normally I’d have kicked any guy out by now, but Evans is different. Confident. Too damn sure of himself. And I’m not stopping him.
He grabs his keys. “Okay, Audrey. I gotta go. I’ll see you soon.”
“Uh-huh.”
“Say it properly,” he teases, hand on the door.
I sigh. “Yes, Evans. I’ll see you.”
“Good.” He flashes one last grin and walks out, leaving me with a stupid smile I didn’t mean to have.

~

“Whatttt—you slept with hot drunky?” Mindy blurts loud enough for the whole bar to hear.
“Hey! Shhh!” I hiss, practically choking on my drink. “Do you want Max to hear?”
“To hell with Max. How the hell did that even happen?”
“I… I don’t know.” I groan.

“Oh please. I saw you two flirting last night. And then poof, both of you vanish after your shift. Don’t tell me…”
“Argh, Mindyyy.” I bury my face in my hands.
“So you did,” she gasps. “Oh my god, you did.”
“Sorry, babe, it’s just… hard to believe,” she says, eyes wide with delight.

I’m already blushing like an idiot when Tara, perched on a stool nearby, chimes in.
“Don’t mind her, Audrey. She’s just jealous.”
“Damn right I’m jealous. Did you see him, Tara? Hot as hell.”
“I bet,” Tara chuckles. “Otherwise our Audrey wouldn’t bother.”
“Shut up. Both of you.”

Tara’s been around since school. These days she’s married, working some fancy pharmaceutical job, doing the whole “successful adult” thing. Good for her. Really, I’m happy she’s sorted her life out. Me? Not so much. She wants to stay in Ashford Hollow forever. I can’t. I won’t. But hey, she’s married, she’s settled. Makes sense for her.

They cackle like it’s the funniest thing in the world. Me, the sarcastic, sharp-mouthed Audrey, reduced to a flustered wreck. God, I need better self-control.

Their voices yank me back when Mindy waves a hand in my face. “Earth to Audrey. So… how was he?”
Tara’s staring too, waiting for my answer.
“I… uh… well…”
Mindy squeals. “That good, huh?”
“Shut up!” I snap, blushing harder.
“I knew it,” Mindy crows. “The second I saw him walk in.”

And of course, perfect timing, Max strolls over.
“What are you girls gossiping about?”
All three of us freeze.
“Well?” Max eyes us.
“Nothing, Maxie. Just Tara’s new boyfriend,” Mindy blurts without missing a beat.
“What?” Tara sputters.
I nearly spit out my drink laughing.
“Thought you were married,” Max frowns.
“She can still date, can’t she?” Mindy grins like the devil.
Max narrows his eyes at Tara. She shoots me a glare before snapping back, “Don’t you have something better to do than harass paying customers?”
“Fine, fine. As long as you’re paying,” Max mutters and walks away.

I snort. “Thank you, Tara.”
“You should thank me,” Mindy winks.
Tara glares at her. “I’ll kill you.”
Mindy just laughs louder, soaking it all in.

We’re still laughing when the door of the empty bar creaks open. And who else but hot drunky himself strolls in.
God must really love watching me squirm.

“That’s him,” Mindy whispers, eyes glued.
Tara’s staring too as Evans walks in like he owns the place. Same brown jacket, same confident stride. And the whole time? His eyes never leave me.

My chest tightens. Breathe, Audrey. It was just a hookup. Just a hookup. So why the hell do I feel like a teenager again?
“Hey, Audrey,” he says, stopping in front of me.
“Hey, Evans.”
And damn it, I’m already blushing.

posts.content:
Author’s Note

Hey everyone, Nin Nin here!

Just a quick update before you dive into Chapter 3. I originally wrote this story back in 10th grade, and while editing and retracing the draft I had in mind, I realized a few things needed to change. I know it’s a little untraditional to tweak earlier chapters after they’re already up, but I want the story to feel right — for me and for you.

So here are the two changes you should know:

  1. Nick’s age isn’t 17 anymore — he’s now 22.
  2. In Chapter 2, Reed does go to the ER, and Nick stays with him through the night.

That’s it! If you’ve already read the first two chapters, you don’t need to go back unless you want to.

Thank you so much for reading and supporting this story — it honestly means a lot. I hope you continue to enjoy Coincidence or Jeanie.

– Nin Nin

To Be Continued

Chapter 3: The First Lead

The ER buzzed with fluorescent lights and nervous chatter. Phones ringing, nurses shuffling, the low hum of people whispering like the walls were listening. I sat there hunched in one of those uncomfortable plastic chairs, staring at my phone.

The note was still there.
Jeanie, Jeanie, Jeanie. Leave me alone.

If I hadn’t written it, maybe I would have known. Maybe I could have stopped this.

I imagined Reed’s car spinning out, glass shattering, his arm slammed against the door. And me? Sitting at home, blissfully “normal,” because I’d told the only thing that ever warned me to shut up.

I sighed, thumb hovering over delete. My hand actually shook. Then I hit it. Gone.

For a second, I just stared at the empty screen. Waiting. Hoping.

And then Reed stirred.

His eyes fluttered open like nothing had happened, and he croaked, “You look like hell.”

A laugh cracked out of me, sharp and tired. “Thanks. You nearly die and I’m the one who looks like hell.”

He smirked weakly. “Guess that makes us even.”

Typical Reed. Survives a car accident, arm in a sling, and still manages to roast me before the IV drip finishes its first cycle.

I knew I should have hugged him. Guy almost died, and here I was still hiding behind sarcasm. Why is it so hard for me to feel anything straight? Why is it easier to crack a joke than admit I care? He’s my best friend, and I’m sitting here like a wall. Then he smiled as his eyes drifted shut, like he knew it too.

I leaned back in the chair, tension finally bleeding out of me. “Don’t scare me like that again.”

“Relax,” he said, eyes half-shut again. “Takes more than a Toyota to kill me.”

I wanted to tell him everything. About the coincidences. The feelings. The name that wouldn’t leave me alone. About how deleting a stupid note somehow brought him back.

But I didn’t.

Instead, I sat there, staring at my best friend breathing, and thought: She’s real. She’s out there. And I need to find her.

~

The morning after Reed’s accident, I tried to act like life was back to normal. Coffee, shower, work. The usual.

Except nothing felt normal.

I couldn’t tell Laura about any of this. Couldn’t tell Reed either. And Dr. McLean? Yeah, right. Even she’d probably smile, nod, and quietly start writing my psych eval.

So instead, I did what any sane, sleep-deprived person would do: I tried to trace Jeanie.

Backtrack the madness. Find where it started.

And then it hit me. The first time I really saw the name wasn’t in a dream, or on a playlist, or whispered in my head. It was online. A short story I’d stumbled across last year on some site called myexperience.com.

The author went by Harmony Hazel.

I didn’t know her. Still don’t. But I remembered liking her story, and in it there was a girl named Jeanie. At the time, I thought nothing of it. Just another character in just another corner of the internet. But now? Now it felt like a thread.

So I sent Harmony an email. Played it casual. Big fan. Loved your story. Thanks for writing.

She replied a day later. Polite. Short. The kind of message you send when you don’t want to keep talking. Glad you enjoyed it. Jeanie’s just a fictional character. Nothing more.

Nothing more.

That should’ve been the end of it. But I didn’t believe her.

Because Jeanie wasn’t “nothing.” She was everywhere in my life. And if Harmony Hazel was the first one to put her name in front of me, then maybe, just maybe, she knew more than she let on.

Or maybe I was going crazy. I don’t know.

Either way, I had to start digging deeper. Research more.

~

That night, I crashed. No tossing, no staring at the ceiling, no paranoia. Just out.

And then came the dream. Again.

Not the same girl as before.

This one had long dark hair, a crown tilted on her head like she was born with it, and wings, massive black wings that stretched wide behind her. She smiled at me, sharp and knowing, like she’d already planned the punchline to a joke I wasn’t in on. Creepy didn’t even cover it.

I jolted awake, pulse racing. Checked the clock. Morning.

Eight hours straight. A miracle.

~

Laura’s laugh carried down the hall, loud enough to rattle my door. I shuffled out, still half-asleep, and found her pacing with her phone pressed to her ear.

“Yes, honey, I can’t wait for the wedding either,” she said, smiling.

Right. The wedding. About a month away.

“Yes, we’ll figure out the house later,” she added.

Which meant she’d be moving out. I was happy for her, I really was, she’d found someone solid. But after a year or two of sharing this place, it’d feel weird without her around. Quiet. Too quiet.

My phone buzzed. Alex.

Now there was a surprise. My other sister rarely calls, too busy with books and competitions and her “future.”

“Wassup, bro,” she said when I picked up.
“Not much. Just woke up. Miraculous, I know.”
“I had to tell you and Laura something, but I guess she’s already on a call.”
“Yeah, fiancé privileges. What’s up?”
“I know I promised I’d visit, but…”
“Let me guess. Work?”
“Not work. A quiz. Inter-college level.”
I smirked. “Ah, the glamorous life of a nerd.”
“Excuse you. If we win, we’re headed to Scotland.”

That caught me. Scotland. The word landed heavier than it should have.

~

Laura’s laugh carried all the way down the hall, loud enough to rattle my door. I dragged myself out of bed, hair sticking up like a failed science experiment, and found her pacing with her phone pressed to her ear.

“Yes, honey, I can’t wait for the wedding either,” she said, smiling like she was already at the altar.

Right. The wedding. Coming up in a month.

“Yes, we’ll figure out the house later,” she added.

Which meant she’d be moving out. I was happy for her, I really was, she’d found someone good, someone solid. But after a year or two of sharing this flat, it’d feel strange without her around. Quiet. Too quiet.

My phone buzzed. Alex. Now that was unusual. My other sister rarely called, too busy with equations and competitions and whatever else future rocket scientists fill their time with.

“Wassup, bro,” she said when I picked up.
“Not much, sis. Just woke up. A modern miracle.”

She laughed softly. “I had to tell you and Laura something, but I guess she’s already on the phone.”
“Yeah, fiancé privileges. So what’s going on?”
“I know I promised I’d visit this week, but…”
I sighed. “Let me guess. Work?”
“Not work. A quiz. Big one. Inter-college level.”
I grinned. “So basically work, but with buzzers.”
“Excuse me, this is serious,” she said, mock-offended. “If we win, it’s straight to Scotland for the next round.”
“Scotland?” I repeated, the word clicking in my head harder than it should have.
“Yep. Scotland. Which means I can’t really sneak away right now.”
“Of course,” I said. “And here I thought you’d blow off international glory just to come hang out with your charming brother.”
She laughed again. “Next time, I promise.”
“Sure, sure. I’ll even run interference with Laura so she doesn’t scold you.”
“You’re the best. Anyway, I think they’re calling me, gotta go.”

Before I could say bye, the line went dead.

I stared at the screen for a second longer, the word still echoing in my head.

Scotland.

posts.title: Under The Cherry Blossoms – Chapter 3: Training
posts.excerpt: The morning sun rouses Lily, encouraging her to greet the day. She dons a chic, professional outfit that reflects her lively spirit and adds a playful touch with a favorite Hello Kitty-adorned bag. After breakfast, she heads to the bus stop, joining the awakening city. During her bus ride, she gazes
posts.content:
The morning sun rouses Lily, encouraging her to greet the day. She dons a chic, professional outfit that reflects her lively spirit and adds a playful touch with a favorite Hello Kitty-adorned bag. After breakfast, she heads to the bus stop, joining the awakening city.

During her bus ride, she gazes at the cityscape, her mind wandering and preparing for the work day ahead. The bus soon stops at her office building. As she gets off, she notices an amusing coincidence - the pink-haired lady from the day before is here again, at the same time. "What are the chances?" she muses. But, prioritizing her work, Lily puts aside her curiosity and enters the building, ready to tackle the day.

As Lily enters the office, Layla, her friend and the cheerful receptionist, greets her with a warm smile. "Good morning, Lily! It's great to see you."

"Good morning, Layla!" Lily replies, returning the smile. "I'm excited for today’s training."

"I bet you are! It's going to be an informative and fun-filled day," Layla says, her enthusiasm contagious.

Lily makes her way to her desk, greeted by the familiar surroundings of her workspace. She takes a moment to settle in, arranging her belongings and mentally preparing herself for the training ahead. The office buzzes with activity as colleagues exchange greetings and prepare for the day's tasks.

As Lily sits at her desk, engrossed in the notes she had meticulously prepared the previous day, she becomes aware of a looming figure behind her. She turns to find a man she doesn't recognize.

"Um, hi. Can I help you?" she ventures, her eyebrows furrowing in slight confusion.

"I've never seen an intern so invested in their work on their very first day," he drawls, his eyes roaming over the papers on her desk.

"Well, technically it's my third day," Lily corrects, trying to place his face. "And you are?"

"Chris," he says simply, tilting his head towards her notes. "Impressive work. Looks like you're taking your training seriously."

"Thank you...I guess?" Lily responds, puzzled by his seemingly backhanded compliment.

"I'm tech support. Sounds funny, right? A tech support guy in a tech company," Chris explains, his tone heavy with a resigned sort of humor. "It's like being a water salesman in an ocean."

"Oh, tech support. Yes, Miss Stone did mention you'd be in touch," Lily recalls, piecing together the puzzle.

"Indeed, about your laptop. Let's get you sorted," Chris invites, gesturing for her to follow him.

As they walk, Lily attempts to make small talk. "So, how many interns do we have?"

Chris raises an eyebrow, "Shouldn't you know? Weren't you at the welcome party?"

"Well, yes, but it's hard to remember everyone after just one meet and greet. I mean, there are over 40 employees," Lily defends, feeling her cheeks heat slightly.

"Yeah, somewhat true. I mean, I wouldn't expect you to remember me because, well, no one does. But the interns are your direct colleagues, so..." Chris trails off, an exaggerated sigh escaping his lips.

"Okay, point taken," Lily concedes, rolling her eyes slightly. His quirkiness was starting to grow on her, despite his somewhat abrasive nature.

Ignoring the playful tension, Chris reveals, "There are three interns, including you."

"Good to know, thanks," Lily replies, deciding to play along with his oddball behavior.

"And based on your notes, I think you will do well compared to the other two doofus," Chris remarks with a smirk.

"Umm, thanks? I think?" Lily responds, unsure whether to take that as a compliment or an insult.

In the supply room, Chris shows her a range of laptops, and she completes the necessary paperwork for her assigned device. She thanks Chris, albeit hesitantly, and returns to her desk.

"What a peculiar guy," she ponders, her initial irritation giving way to a sense of intrigue about Chris's unusual persona.

Returning to her desk, Lily spots Ethan casually standing nearby. On seeing her, he gives a polite nod, "Hi Lily, hope you're settling in well."

"Hi Mr. Harris, yes, I'm adjusting well, thank you," Lily replies in kind.

"Please, call me Ethan. We're beyond the formalities," he responds playfully.

"Sure, Ethan," Lily responds, a hint of amusement in her voice.

"You ready for the training?" Ethan queries, a hint of professional curiosity in his tone.

"Yes, I'm eager to get started," Lily responds, her enthusiasm apparent.

"Great, follow me," Ethan instructs, leading the way towards the conference room. Lily trails behind him, her attention briefly caught by the other interns, Aisha and Daniel. They each acknowledge Lily with a nod, to which she responds with a polite smile.

"As I assume you've already met the rest of the team yesterday?" Ethan inquires, keeping up casual conversation as they navigate the office.

"Yes, I met them at the welcome party. Everyone seems really friendly," Lily responds, recalling the warm reception she'd received.

"Indeed, we have a good team here. For this new project, you'll be working closely with Aisha and Daniel. They're a bit ahead since they completed their training last month, but I'm sure you'll catch up," Ethan explains, offering words of encouragement.

As they enter the conference room, Lily takes in the professional setup - a large conference table in the middle and a mounted presentation board on one wall.

"I'm confident I'll catch up soon," Lily assures, her voice filled with determination.

"Good," Ethan acknowledges with a nod. "Now, did you bring along the files I prepared for your first task?" he asks, taking a seat and gesturing for Lily to do the same.

"Yes, I have them here. I've also prepared some additional notes," Lily says, her eagerness evident as she takes a seat beside Ethan.

"That's interesting," Ethan comments, a spark of curiosity in his eyes.

Lily hands over her notes. They are meticulous and well-prepared. Ethan's brow furrows slightly as he reads them, appreciating her efforts. "This is impressive work, Lily. You've done well," he compliments.

"So you do smile," Lily teases lightly, noticing the slight curve of his lips.

Caught off guard, Ethan swiftly regains his composure. A small, lingering smile remains.

"From what I've seen so far, I have high expectations for you. I trust you'll make a valuable contribution to our team," Ethan remarks, his trust in Lily palpable.

"I won't let you down," Lily promises, her resolve clear.

In the conference room, Ethan delves into the complex product they're developing, expounding upon Lily's crucial role in coordination and support. His fluent breakdown of the project elevates Lily's respect for him, both on a personal and professional level.

Ethan painstakingly outlines Lily's duties, stressing that her project management abilities will be instrumental to the project's success, more so than technical prowess. As Lily pens diligent notes, she begins to envisage the ways in which she can facilitate the team's efforts.

Regardless of her growing attraction to Ethan, Lily maintains her professionalism. His charm both spurs and intrigues her, and she values the opportunity to learn from his mentorship. As the meeting wraps up, Lily experiences a swirl of professional ambition and personal interest, eager to prove her worth and make a significant contribution to the project.

As the initial training session concludes, Ethan expresses his approval of Lily's active participation and comprehension of the material.

"You've shown a good understanding of the material, Lily, especially considering you're not from a tech background," Ethan commends, offering her an approving nod.

"Thank you, Ethan," Lily replies, gathering her notes and belongings from the table.

"The project you'll be working on, Raviga, is a kind of a pilot project," Ethan clarifies, giving Lily additional insight. "The actual project is being managed by other team members, but I'll also be assigning it to you so that we can gauge your performance at the end of the training period and compare your work to that of the main team."

"So the project I'll be working on won't have real-world implications?" Lily inquires, seeking further clarification.

"No, Lily, that's not the case," Ethan reassures her. "The project does hold value. We're designating it as a pilot project for the duration of your training period. It serves as a benchmark to assess how well you handle tasks that align with our team's work. Once the training period concludes, you'll be assigned actual projects that directly contribute to our ongoing work."

Lily digests Ethan's explanation and responds, "Understood, Ethan."

"Here at the company, we conduct performance reviews on a quarterly basis for both regular employees, however for interns it’s monthly," Ethan explains.

"That’s interesting," Lily remarks, intrigued by the constant feedback loop.

"Absolutely. While these reviews don't impact your job security as such, they will play a crucial role in the decision-making process of whether to onboard you as a regular employee post your three-month internship. However, based on your performance in our first training session, I have a feeling you'll do well," Ethan shares, projecting a confidence that instills a sense of reassurance in Lily.

"I certainly hope to live up to your expectations, Ethan," Lily responds, already resolved to give this opportunity her all.

"Your training and the remainder of your internship will be carefully evaluated. And remember, if you have any queries or need assistance with anything, you can always approach me," Ethan further assures.

"I will, Ethan," Lily affirms, appreciating his approachable demeanor.

"Alright then, we've had a productive day. Can't believe it's already lunchtime," Ethan glances at the clock, the morning having seemingly flown by.

"Haha, yes, it's the most awaited part of the day," Lily adds humorously, her eyes lighting up at the mention of food.

"I guess for some it might be," Ethan chuckles, sharing in the light-hearted banter. As Lily starts packing her laptop and notes into her bag, Ethan's attention is drawn to the Hello Kitty emblem on it. He grins and comments, "So, you're a fan of Hello Kitty?"

"Haha, yes-yes, she's quite adorable," Lily replies, her smile just as infectious.

"Good to know Lily is fond of cute things," Ethan teases good-naturedly.

"Isn't everyone?" Lily shoots back, her enthusiasm never faltering.

"True enough," Ethan concedes, a thoughtful look on his face.

Just as Lily prepares to rise from her chair, her pen slips from her hand, rolling under the table. Both Ethan and Lily bend down to retrieve it simultaneously, resulting in an unexpected proximity. A moment of awkward realization passes between them, and they quickly retreat, their faces flush. "I'll see you post lunch, Lily," Ethan says, breaking the moment before making a swift exit. With her pen in hand and a soft smile on her face, Lily heads out of the conference room, her mind already buzzing with anticipation for the journey ahead.

To Be Continued on Chapter 4

posts.title: Audrey -- Chapter 2 : Mistake or Worth it?
posts.excerpt: Looking back, I do realize I’ve made a lot of mistakes. Impulsive ones. Stupid ones. The kind that make you wake up at three in the morning and groan into your pillow. But sometimes? Living in the moment feels worth it. My problem is I tend to stay in those moments way too long. ~ “Drunk idiot.” Max
posts.content:
Looking back, I do realize I’ve made a lot of mistakes. Impulsive ones. Stupid ones. The kind that make you wake up at three in the morning and groan into your pillow. But sometimes? Living in the moment feels worth it. My problem is I tend to stay in those moments way too long.

~

“Drunk idiot.” Max sighs so loud it could pass for a death rattle.
Mindy’s practically crying with laughter while I crouch down to check the guy’s ID.

Evans Carter.
Even his picture looks good, which is unfair, because most IDs make you look like a prison escapee. Mine? I look like a witch who curses traffic cops for fun.

“Audrey,” Max says.
“Mm?” I hum, not looking up.
“Call John and Casey. Get this one carried out before he decides the floor’s his mattress.”
“Oh, come on, Max. He just passed out. It’s not even closing time yet.”
“Exactly. Which is why I don’t want him drooling here all night.”
I smirk. “Sure. But if you kick him out now, how do you plan on getting paid?”

That makes him pause. Money always does.
He sighs. “Fine. Couch. At least move him where people don’t have to step over him.”
“What people?” I glance around the nearly empty bar. Charles and his creepy buddy are the only ones left, nursing their drinks like gargoyles. “Our VIP clientele?”
Max gives me that look.
“Alright, alright.” I hold up my hands. “I’ll call John. We’ll move your precious drunk before he stains the floor.”
“Thank you.” Max sighs again, dramatic as always.

The guy’s out cold for a while. A couple customers wander in, I serve drinks, chat with Mindy, and every so often we glance over at him, still dead to the world on the couch like a log.
Eventually he stirs, blinking up at the ceiling, shaking his head like he’s trying to remember what planet he’s on. Country music twangs from the speakers as his eyes dart around, totally lost.

I grab a glass of water and head over.
“Wakey wakey, sunshine,” I say.
“Uh… hi?” He looks at me, confused.
“Don’t remember who I am?” I tease.
“I—well—” he stammers, still trying to piece it together.
“Relax,” I laugh. “I’m just the bartender. You drank yourself stupid and passed out for a while.”
“Oh.” He looks completely dumbfounded.
“Rough night?” I ask.
“You could say that,” he mutters.
“Here. Hydrate before you collapse again.” I hand him the glass.
“Thanks,” he says, taking a sip.

And of course, right on cue, Max shows up.
“Now that you’ve enjoyed our hospitality,” he says, “you don’t mind paying for it, do you?”
Jesus. This man and his timing. I shake my head.

“Huh? Yes, sir.” The guy fumbles for his wallet immediately.
“Audrey,” Max says, looking at me. “Get the bill. Charge him extra for using the bar as a hotel.” Then, to the guy: “You don’t mind, right?”
“I—uh—yeah, whatever it is, I’ll pay.”
Max just smiles that smug little smile and walks away.

The guy, Evans, hands me a card. It’s odd. Not a card I’ve ever seen before. But whatever. I take it and head back to run the payment.
After I run the payment, the guy takes his card back.
“Thanks for the water,” he says.
“Oh, it’s fine.”
He rubs his temple. “I don’t really remember much of… any of this. What even happened?”
“You were drunk as fuck, that’s what happened.”
“Yeah, that explains the headache,” he mutters.

I watch him pull his brown jacket on, still looking half out of it. I smirk. “Hold up.”
He blinks at me as I duck into the breakroom, grab the aspirin bottle, and come back. I shake two into my hand and pass them over.
“What’s this?” he asks.
“Aspirin. Might help with the whole ‘my brain is splitting in half’ thing.”
“Oh. Thanks.”
“Audrey,” I say, nodding at him.
“Evans.”
“I know,” I shoot back.

He looks at me, a little surprised, then turns and heads for the door.

~

The next day is the same old story. No customers. Max grumbling about how they’ll show up “any minute now.” Mindy glued to her phone, flirting with some guy who probably thinks she’s a model. The only silver lining? Charles and his pervy sidekick don’t make an appearance.

“Less creeps to deal with,” I say.
“Aww, missing them?” Mindy grins.
“Sure, if I feel like puking.”

Honestly, weekdays here aren’t terrible. The pay’s decent, shifts are quieter, and Max somehow keeps the place running off the weekend rush. But bartending forever? Not happening.

I’m mid-thought when the door swings open. A guy walks in, and I look up, then freeze.

Evans.
Except not Evans-from-last-night. This Evans is upright, sober, in a clean jacket and jeans. Hair combed, face awake. The kind of glow-up that makes you wonder if you hallucinated the drunk mess from before.

I glance down at myself, same black bartender dress I wear every night here. Still hot, I think, adjusting my posture as he heads straight for me.
“Evening, Audrey.”
I look up. “Evans, right?”
He smirks. “Glad you remember.”
“Hard to forget the guy who passed out on my floor. You look… less tragic tonight.”
“Thanks. I clean up well when I’m not competing for town drunk of the year.”
“Uh-huh. So what do you want? Drink? Or just here to nap again?”
He leans on the counter, all fake casual. “Actually, I came to thank the bartender who saved me with water and aspirin.”
I arch a brow. “Cute. But Max doesn’t let anyone loiter. Buy something or he’ll have you tossed.”

Evans glances at Max glaring from across the room. “Whiskey, then. On the rocks.”
“Good choice.” I pour, slide it over. “So… now that you’ve paid your entry fee, what’s on your mind?”
“You.”
I snort. “Wow. Straight to it, huh? Gonna try to pick me up with that line?”
“Not trying. Just talking. I’m interested.”
I roll my eyes. “You’re wasting your time. I’m not exactly fascinating.”
“For me, you are.”

I hate it. I hate how smooth he sounds. I hate how he’s looking at me like I’m the only one in the room. And most of all, I hate that it’s working.
“I’m not interested,” I tell him flat.
He smirks. “You sure about that? Give me a chance.”
“To the guy who face-planted in my bar?”
“Fair point. Not my best night. But I’m better company sober.”
“Uh-huh. Do you toss these lines at every girl?”
“Only the ones worth it.”
“Oh, wow. I’m swooning.”

He sips his whiskey, eyes on me like I’m the only thing in the room. “Hazel eyes. Gorgeous.”
I bark out a laugh. “Wow, straight from the Hallmark aisle.”
He laughs too, and for a second it almost feels real. I catch Mindy watching from the side, practically drooling.
“Maybe hit on her instead,” I say, nodding toward her.
He doesn’t even glance over. “She didn’t hand me water and aspirin.”

Okay. That was smooth. I hate that it was smooth.
“Kindness’ll get you in trouble,” I say. “World doesn’t exactly reward it.”
“Still. You did it.”

This guy is trouble. I can feel it. So I change lanes.
“So what was with the blackout? Breakup? Bar fight?”
“Drama,” he says simply. Then, after a pause: “Couldn’t ignore it.”
“I get that.”
His eyes flick back to mine. “Can’t ignore you either.”

And there it is again, too direct, too confident, and way too effective.
“Listen,” I cut in. “This little flirting thing? Cute. But I’m not interested.”
He grins like he didn’t even hear me. “We’d look good together.”
I snap, “Evans, not in a million years.”

~

And yet, look at me now. Judge away. Hell, I’m judging myself.
It’s morning. I’m naked. And next to me? Evans Carter, fast asleep like he owns the place.

Don’t ask me how he talked me into it, because I honestly don’t know. I’ve made a lot of bad, impulsive choices. This one? Add it to the pile.
Was it worth it? The sex? Yeah. I’m not gonna lie.
But it’s never the sex that ruins you. It’s what comes after.

To Be Continued on Chapter 3

posts.title: Under The Cherry Blossoms - Chapter 2: Welcome Party
posts.excerpt: Excitement sparked in Lily's heart as she dressed for her first day as an intern. She took the bus, and as the office building loomed into view, she noticed a woman who was impossible to ignore. The woman's hair was an eye-catching shade of pink, flamboyantly defying her naturally blonde roots. Dres
posts.content:
Excitement sparked in Lily's heart as she dressed for her first day as an intern. She took the bus, and as the office building loomed into view, she noticed a woman who was impossible to ignore.

The woman's hair was an eye-catching shade of pink, flamboyantly defying her naturally blonde roots. Dressed in a pristine cream coat paired with a crisp white shirt and a tailored black skirt, she exuded an aura that turned heads. "Who could she be?" Lily wondered, her curiosity piqued. With a mental shrug, she turned her focus back to her first day, marching past the woman and into the building.

"Good morning, Lily," chirped Layla, the receptionist, her smile as warm as a sunbeam.

Returning her greeting with an equally cheerful, "Good morning, Layla," Lily approached the front desk.

"How'd you find your first day?" Layla asked, leaning forward with genuine interest.

"Pretty standard orientation stuff, nothing too challenging. Although..." Lily paused for dramatic effect, "... the espresso was a revelation."

Layla's laugh rang out, a pleasant sound in the quiet office. "Tell me about it! That espresso machine keeps me sane. Sometimes I even come in on holidays just for a cup."

Lily laughed along, promising, "I might just follow in your footsteps."

Layla winked at her, adding, "And remember, if you need anything, just give me a shout. Melissa's in her office too. As for your assignments, that's Mr. Harris's territory."

Lily's brows shot up. "Mr. Harris works individually with all the staff?"

"Indeed, he does. The man's practically a machine. Never seen him crack a smile or join in a laugh. But when he gets down to business... you can't help but pay attention." Layla leaned in closer, her voice dropping to a hushed whisper. "Plus, well... he's pretty hot."

A surprised laugh bubbled up from Lily, "Sounds like someone has a bit of a crush on our boss."

"Crush, no. Admiration for his looks, absolutely. But let's not get confused here. As attractive as he might be, he's still a workaholic tool," Layla quipped, a mischievous gleam in her eye.

"Hmm, can't wait to meet him," Lily said, her tone dry.

"You'll see for yourself soon enough. Oh, one more thing you should know about starting here..." Layla's insider information was cut short by a sharp rebuke from across the room.

"Hey, this isn't a coffee club! Get back to work!" Melissa called out from her office, her voice stern.

"Sorry, Melissa," Layla murmured, her brow furrowed.

Apologizing quickly, Lily added, "Yes, Miss Stone. I better get to my desk."

Layla grinned sheepishly, nodding. "Yeah, catch you later, Lily."

As Lily navigated the sea of cubicles to her desk, she noticed the neighboring seat was conspicuously empty. "Where's Liam?" she wondered, her brow furrowing as she settled into her chair. She was greeted by a pile of papers stacked neatly on her desk. Each sheet was crammed with project details, bulleted responsibilities, and a roadmap of deliverables, the precision of which was surprising, even for an intern position. A frisson of excitement coursed through her, mingling with a hint of trepidation. "This is the real deal," she thought, letting out a soft chuckle as she skimmed through the daunting workload.

"Interesting, isn't it?" A voice teased from behind her, and she turned to find Liam, a knowing smile playing on his lips.

"Yeah," Lily admitted, her eyes gleaming with enthusiasm, "I've done internships before, but nothing this... detailed. It's quite impressive."

A chuckle rumbled from Liam's throat. "That's Mr. Harris for you. He personally crafts these project outlines. The man's dedication is contagious, that's why everyone here pushes themselves."

Lily nodded, a newfound respect for their enigmatic boss forming. "I can see that."

Liam continued, "And of course, every new hire undergoes a mandatory training period, provided by Mr. Harris himself. Afterward, you'll be officially up and running."

Her eyes widened slightly at this revelation. "Training? How long does that usually last?"

"Typically a month," Liam responded. "Mr. Harris will brief you in detail, though he seems to be running a bit late today."

Lily hummed in acknowledgment, her mind swirling with anticipation and a dash of nervousness.

"Oh, and one more thing," Liam added, a playful grin tugging at his lips. "As a sort of tradition, Mr. Harris personally hosts a welcome lunch for all the new recruits. It's his way of making us feel included."

Lily's eyebrows shot up, surprise etched on her features. "Really? When is it?"

"Actually, it's today," Liam replied, the corners of his mouth curling into a wider grin. "Mr. Harris likes to hold it on the second day of newbies' arrival. Should be around noon."

Her heart hammered in her chest, excitement coursing through her. The prospect of meeting the entire team, along with the much-discussed Mr. Harris, held a thrilling promise. From what she'd gathered so far, this job was going to be anything but ordinary.

Lily found herself lost in her work throughout the day. She scrutinized all the files regarding the assigned company project, gaining a comprehensive understanding of the data and requirements. The office was a hive of activity, with colleagues engaged in lively discussions and shared idea sessions. Admiration swelled within Lily as she took in the dynamic atmosphere of Mystique Holdings - a workspace where passion seemed to fuel everyone's work ethic.

As noon approached, she was startled out of her work bubble by Melissa's announcement. "Attention, everyone! As per our tradition, Mr. Harris has decided to throw a lunch for every staff member in honor of the arrival of our newest and youngest team member, Lily Andrews."

A burst of applause filled the room, all eyes turning toward Lily. She smiled at the warm reception, already appreciating the welcoming work culture.

"As always, we're heading to Bolton's," Melissa continued. "Let's get moving, people!"

The room was instantly abuzz as everyone began to rise from their desks, Lily amongst them.

"I told you about the tradition," Liam chimed in with a beaming smile.

"You sure did," Lily replied, tidying her desk. "How are we getting there, though?"

"Everyone's driving or carpooling," he answered.

"I see. I guess I'll need to book a cab since I don't have a vehicle," she frowned, pulling out her phone.

An excited glint shone in Liam's eyes. "You know, I have a bike. You could—"

But before he could finish his sentence, Layla made her energetic presence known. "Lilyyy!" she exclaimed, rushing over and enveloping Lily in a hug. "Come on, you're riding with me."

"Huh?" Lily blinked, taken aback.

"Of course! You're the only one here without wheels, so it was a no-brainer. We're already late, and Mr. Harris is already there. Plus, we're taking the ever-so-grumpy HR along too," she added hastily.

A stern voice echoed behind them. "Who are you calling grumpy again, Layla?" It was Melissa, her hands on her hips.

Layla went pale, a stark contrast to her usually vibrant demeanor. "Nothing, nothing at all. I'll get the car ready for both of you," she deflected swiftly, darting off towards the door.

"That girl," Melissa muttered, shaking her head. She turned to Lily, her voice softer. "Let's go, Lily."

"Well, looks like I have a ride. Sorry about that, Liam, you were saying?" Lily smiled at him, a touch of amusement in her eyes.

Liam gave her a slightly deflated smile. "Oh, it was nothing, Lily. I'll see you at Bolton's, okay?"

"Um, sure, Liam," she nodded, following Melissa out, leaving a disgruntled Liam in her wake.

"Darn it, Layla," Liam muttered under his breath.

Upon arriving at Bolton's, Lily was instantly struck by the vibrant atmosphere that enveloped the party hall. The air was filled with jovial chatter and laughter, creating an overwhelming mix of excitement and anxiety within her. Guided through the crowd by Melissa, Lily was introduced to various team members, each face more unfamiliar than the last. Despite her best efforts, the buzz of the atmosphere made remembering names quite the challenge.

A towering figure clad in a sleek, black suit emerges from the crowd. His muscular build and beard add to his commanding presence. "Hello, newbie," he greets.

"Um, hi," Lily responds, taken aback.

"Lily, this is our new intern. Frederick, we're throwing this welcome party for her," Melissa interjects.

"I'm aware," Frederick responds.

"Lily, allow me to introduce Frederick Henderson, one of the co-founders of Mystique Holdings," Melissa presents Frederick to Lily.

"Hello, Lily. I hope you're enjoying your welcome party," Frederick says, a warm smile spreading across his face.

"I am, thank you," Lily replies politely, trying to match his ease.

"Good to hear. I'll let you get back to it. Enjoy your day." Frederick raises his drink in a brief toast before he takes his leave.

Eventually, they navigated their way to a large table in the middle of the room, reserved for their group. Familiar faces orbited around the table, including Liam, who must have arrived earlier thanks to his bike. His enthusiastic wave was a welcome sight amid the sea of strangers. As they advanced towards the table, another familiar face drew Lily's attention.

There, at the center of the room, stood Ethan, engaged in lively conversation with a few individuals. His black suit complimented his black hair and fair complexion. His confident demeanor and magnetic charm were impossible to ignore, earning him the rapt attention of the surrounding crowd. His charisma was palpable, leaving an indelible mark on everyone present.

Caught off guard by the sight of him, Lily turned to Melissa, asking, "Wait, Miss Stone, who is that?" She pointed at Ethan.

"That's Mr. Harris," Melissa responded with a knowing smile. "Our manager you've heard so much about."

"He's our manager?" Lily was taken aback, her eyes wide with surprise.

"Yes," Melissa chuckled. "Most are surprised at first. He may look young, but he's not as young as you'd think. Owning a company of this size at his age is quite an achievement. You know what, let me go get him for you."

"No, no, that's not necessary!" Lily stammered, but her words were drowned out by Melissa's determined strides towards Ethan.

"But that's not why I'm shocked," Lily thought to herself, her mind still processing the revelation. Spotting Layla nearby, she called out, " Oi, pssst! Layla! Over here!"

Layla quickly made her way over, asking, "What's up, Lily?"

"He's our boss? The owner of Mystique Holdings?" Lily gestured towards Ethan, who Melissa was now fetching.

"Yes, indeed! That's our handsome boss. Quite the looker, isn't he?" Layla exclaimed enthusiastically. "Told you so!"

"You said he was a workaholic tool, who never smiles or seems to enjoy anything," Lily countered.

"And he is. In all my three years here, I swear I've never seen him smile," Layla insisted.

"But that can't be right. When I met him, he did smile," Lily blurted out without thinking.

"Hold on, you've met him before? And you've seen him smile?" Layla looked astounded.

"Um, kind of," Lily replied, suddenly regretting her admission.

"I need all the details, girl! Spill it!" Layla pleaded.

"How about we save that for later? Miss Stone is bringing him over to me right now," Lily suggested, successfully diverting Layla's attention.

Meanwhile, Ethan spotted Melissa trying to approach him while he was deeply engrossed in a conversation with two gentlemen. Spotting Melissa, he waved her over and said, "Ah, perfect timing, Melissa. Let me introduce you to Mr. Travis and Mr. Raymond. They are our top clients for our product."

Melissa came forward and shook hands with both the men. "Oh, I already know them, Ethan. It's nice to finally meet you both in person."

Then, Melissa turned her attention back to Ethan, reminding him, "Ethan, we have our new intern waiting. This welcome party is for her, after all." She then turned to the two men, adding, "If you'll excuse us, gentlemen, I need to borrow him for a few minutes."

"Of course. We'll be here," Mr. Travis replied.

Ethan quickly arranged for drinks for the two gentlemen before following Melissa away from the group. "Thank you, Melissa. Those two can be a bit much," he admitted.

Melissa simply chuckled. "That's because you prefer to avoid these public dealings," she remarked.

"Right. So, where is our new intern?" Ethan asked, looking around the room.

"There she is," Melissa pointed out, directing his gaze towards Lily.

Ethan's eyes widened slightly in recognition as he spotted Lily awkwardly standing at a distance, not sure how to react. A small chuckle escaped him, realizing that the new intern was the same girl he had helped just the day before.

Nearby, Layla noticed his reaction and turned to Lily in surprise. "Holy crap!! Did he just... smile? That's a first," she murmured.

Lily quickly hushed her, not wanting to draw attention to the conversation. But it was too late, Ethan and Melissa were already approaching them.

As Ethan and Melissa approached Lily and Layla, Lily could feel her heart beating faster. She tried to steady her nerves, all the while noticing the flicker of amusement on Ethan's face that suggested he recognized her from their previous encounter. Layla, on the other hand, was having a tough time containing her excitement at witnessing Ethan's seldom-seen smile.

"Hello again, Lily," Ethan greeted, his lingering smile adding a layer of friendliness to his tone. "Seems like we have a knack for running into each other. I trust you found yesterday's meal satisfactory?"

Feeling a mix of embarrassment and curiosity, Lily blushed slightly. "Yes, it was quite a day. Thank you again for your help with my scarf...and the food, of course."

Ethan let out a soft chuckle, his eyes twinkling with amusement. "Not a problem at all. I'm glad I could assist. And hello, Layla," he pivoted, addressing the bubbly receptionist who was barely containing her delight.

"Yes, that's me. We haven't really had much interaction outside of work, Mr. Harris," Layla acknowledged, trying to suppress her smile.

"That's true, Layla. Between managing the company and attending to other responsibilities, it can be challenging to find time for social interactions," Ethan replied with a polite smile.

"That's understandable—" Layla started to respond, but was promptly interrupted by Melissa.

"Well, I think I'll leave you three to get better acquainted. Enjoy the party, everyone! And Lily, remember, don't hesitate to ask Ethan any questions or share your thoughts. He's here to support you during your internship," Melissa interjected, picking up on the budding camaraderie among them.

With a friendly nod, Melissa excused herself and left to mingle with the other partygoers, leaving Lily, Layla, and Ethan in a lively conversation, the ambient music adding to the overall energy of the venue.

Unable to contain her curiosity any longer, Layla bursts with excitement. "Okay, hold on. You two have definitely met before. What's the backstory here?"

Lily gives Ethan a quick glance, her feelings a cocktail of nervousness and intrigue. Opting to share an abbreviated version of their encounter, she carefully sidesteps revealing too much. "Well, Layla, it's not as fascinating as you think. We ran into each other on the street and Ethan helped me recover my lost scarf."

A playful eyebrow raises on Layla's face. "Hmm, a random meeting, huh? Well, looks like fate decided to have a bit of fun by bringing you two together again."

Ethan chuckles lightly, his eyes locking with Lily's, and admits, "Indeed, it certainly seems that way."

Caught off guard by Ethan's chuckle, Layla exclaims, "He chuckled! Seriously, I've been here three years, and I've never heard him laugh before."

Ethan, still chuckling, responds, "Haha, that's a bit of an exaggeration, Layla. I do find humor in things from time to time." Flashing a charming smile at Lily, he adds with a hint of flirtation, "Especially when I'm surrounded by good company, which I definitely am right now with the two of you."

Layla squeals in delight, while Lily blushes at Ethan's flirtatious comment. Intrigued and slightly overwhelmed by his presence, she realizes that there's more to Ethan than meets the eye. Gathering her courage, she asks, "So, Mr. Harris, may I ask how old you are? Miss Stone mentioned you're quite young for someone who owns such a substantial company."

"Oh, I'm 32, turning 33 later this year," Ethan replies, shifting his attention to Lily. "And you, Miss Andrews, how old are you?"

"I'm 21, and I'll be 22 later this year," Lily answers.

"Ah, got it. And Layla, you're 24, correct?" Ethan asks.

"That's right. I'm impressed you remember," Layla replies.

"Haha, well, remembering my employees is part of being a good manager," Ethan says with a smile.

Eager to continue the conversation, Lily asks, "So, Ethan, what does your role as manager involve exactly? I'm trying to get a handle on the breadth of my internship."

Leaning against a nearby table, Ethan meets Lily's gaze thoughtfully and begins to explain. "My role involves overseeing the operations of our company, managing various projects, and ensuring that everything runs smoothly. I also work closely with our clients and collaborate with different teams to drive growth and success."

Lily nods, taking in the information, and responds, "That sounds challenging but thrilling. I'm looking forward to learning from you and the rest of the team."

Ethan offers a warm smile. "I'm glad to hear that, Lily. We have a fantastic team here, and I believe your fresh perspective and enthusiasm will be valuable additions."

Layla watches the back-and-forth between them with playful interest, intrigued by Ethan's more relaxed demeanor. She has never seen him interact with an employee outside of work so casually. The rapport between Lily and Ethan seems unique compared to his other interactions.

As the evening nears its end, Ethan bids them goodbye, leaving Lily with a whirlwind of emotions and a head full of questions about the blossoming connection between them. "I'll leave you two to enjoy the rest of the evening. Welcome to the team, Lily. I'm looking forward to working with you," he says before blending back into the crowd.

"So, spill the tea, Lily! How did you two really meet?" Layla asks, barely containing her excitement.

Lily chuckles, feeling a warmth in her heart for her newfound friend. "It's quite a story, Layla," she begins, but Layla interrupts with enthusiasm.

"We've got all the time in the world," Layla says, motioning for Lily to join her on a nearby seat.

Lily takes a deep breath, settling down and starting to recount the events, from the lost scarf to their unexpected encounter at burger king. Layla listens intently, her eyes wide with intrigue. The more Lily shares, the more fascinated Layla becomes. By the time Lily finishes her story, Layla is practically bouncing with excitement.

"That's incredible! A chance meeting that brought you both here, to the same company," Layla chuckles.

Lily smiles, acknowledging the improbability of it all. "Yes, it does sound unreal."

"But Lily, it's not just about the story," Layla says, her voice turning serious. "It's also about how Ethan interacted with you. Trust me, in all the years I've known him, I've never seen him show such warmth to anyone. It's intriguing."

Lily looks at Layla, her mind spinning with thoughts. Ethan is becoming a mystery she's eager to unravel, an enigma she wants to understand.

As the party begins to wind down, Lily takes a moment to absorb the ambiance. The twinkling lights, the soft murmurs of conversation. After spending time with her colleagues and enjoying the festivities, she decides it's time to head home.

With a final hug to Layla, Lily steps out of Bolton's into the cool night air, realizing that the hours have flown by since their arrival at noon. The bustling energy of the party gradually fades into the background, replaced by the distant hum of city life and the gentle rustling of swaying trees in the evening breeze.

She begins her walk towards the bus stop, her footsteps echoing on the sidewalk. The city lights, reflecting off the buildings around her, seem to dance in harmony with her racing thoughts. Amidst the sea of people returning home from work, she finds herself lost in introspection, each step taking her deeper into her own musings.

Her mind drifts back to the past two days – her first day at work, the unexpected encounter with Ethan, and the vibrant office party. It all feels surreal, like a whirlwind of experiences. Meeting Ethan had been a surprise, stirring up a mix of emotions within her. His captivating features, the underlying warmth beneath his professional exterior, and the unexplained sense of familiarity she felt around him – it all adds to the intrigue.

As the bus stop comes into view, its dimly lit sign contrasting with her luminous thoughts, Lily finds herself replaying her conversations with Ethan in her mind. His words, his gestures, and the way his eyes sparkled with a smile – everything about him holds an unexplainable allure.

She keeps returning to his parting words, "Welcome to the team, Lily. I look forward to working with you." His voice, genuine and warm, sends a delightful shiver down her spine, igniting a sense of anticipation for what lies ahead.

Finally settling into a corner seat on the bus, she gazes out the window at the passing cityscape.

 The excitement of her new job, her growing curiosity about Ethan, and the promise of a fulfilling journey fill her with a deep sense of contentment.

As the bus turns a corner, she catches a glimpse of the office building, bathed in the glow of city lights. A surge of determination washes over her, reinforcing her readiness to embrace this new chapter in her life and confront any challenges that come her way.

Arriving at her stop, she makes her way home. The warm glow of her porch light welcomes her like an old friend. Slipping off her heels, lost in her thoughts, she fails to notice the relief that washes over her tired feet.

As she lies down on her bed, a smile tugs at the corners of her lips, replaying the events of the day like a movie in her mind. The excitement, the nerves, the unexpected friendship, and, most importantly, Ethan. He's become a prominent presence in her thoughts and dreams, leaving her wondering what the future holds for them.

To Be Continued on Chapter 3
